content.text: Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) is Google's open-source Python framework for building, testing, and deploying multi-agent systems on top of Gemini models. ADK's central design principle is that agents are
: any agent can be wrapped as a tool and called by a parent orchestrator, enabling arbitrarily deep agent hierarchies.
ADK also ships first-party integrations with Google Cloud tooling — built-in search, code execution, and one-step deployment to Vertex AI Agent Engine.
Set your Gemini API key before running:
. For Vertex AI models set up Application Default Credentials instead.

import asyncio
from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent
from google.adk.runners import Runner
from google.adk.sessions import InMemorySessionService
from google.genai import types as genai_types

# Define the agent
agent = LlmAgent(
    name="assistant",
    model="gemini-2.0-flash-exp",
    instruction="You are a concise, helpful assistant. Answer in one sentence.",
)

# Create a session service and runner
session_service = InMemorySessionService()
runner = Runner(agent=agent, app_name="my_app", session_service=session_service)

async def main():
    session = await session_service.create_session(app_name="my_app", user_id="user1")
    user_message = genai_types.Content(
        role="user",
        parts=[genai_types.Part(text="What is the capital of Japan?")],
    )
    async for event in runner.run_async(
        user_id="user1", session_id=session.id, new_message=user_message
    ):
        if event.is_final_response():
            print(event.content.parts[0].text)

asyncio.run(main())
The runner emits a stream of events per turn. Only events where
is true carry the agent's text output.
to expose it to the agent. ADK reads the function's type annotations and docstring to build the tool schema sent to Gemini.

from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent
from google.adk.tools import FunctionTool

def get_current_weather(city: str) -> dict:
    """Get the current weather for a city.

    Args:
        city: Name of the city to query.

    Returns:
        A dictionary with temperature_celsius and condition keys.
    """
    weather_db = {
        "Tokyo": {"temperature_celsius": 22, "condition": "sunny"},
        "London": {"temperature_celsius": 14, "condition": "cloudy"},
        "Paris": {"temperature_celsius": 19, "condition": "partly cloudy"},
    }
    return weather_db.get(city, {"temperature_celsius": 20, "condition": "unknown"})

# Wrap the function as a FunctionTool
weather_tool = FunctionTool(func=get_current_weather)

agent = LlmAgent(
    name="weather_agent",
    model="gemini-2.0-flash-exp",
    instruction="Help users get weather information. Use the get_current_weather tool.",
    tools=[weather_tool],
)
sections) — ADK parses them to populate the tool's description, which guides the model's selection decisions.
ADK ships several first-party tools that integrate with Google services.

from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent
from google.adk.tools import google_search, code_execution

agent = LlmAgent(
    name="research_agent",
    model="gemini-2.0-flash-exp",
    instruction=(
        "You are a research assistant. Search the web for current information "
        "and execute Python code to perform calculations when needed."
    ),
    tools=[google_search, code_execution],
)
Grounded web search via Google Search API
Execute Python in a sandboxed environment
Enterprise search over Vertex AI datastores
list to create a hierarchy. The parent agent can delegate tasks to sub-agents by name.

from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent

# Specialist sub-agents
researcher = LlmAgent(
    name="researcher",
    model="gemini-2.0-flash-exp",
    instruction=(
        "You are a research specialist. When given a topic, provide a concise "
        "factual summary using only well-established information."
    ),
    tools=[],
)

writer = LlmAgent(
    name="writer",
    model="gemini-2.0-flash-exp",
    instruction=(
        "You are a professional writer. Turn factual summaries into polished, "
        "engaging prose suitable for a general audience."
    ),
    tools=[],
)

# Orchestrator that delegates to specialists
orchestrator = LlmAgent(
    name="orchestrator",
    model="gemini-2.0-flash-exp",
    instruction=(
        "You coordinate a research and writing pipeline. "
        "First use the researcher sub-agent to gather facts, "
        "then use the writer sub-agent to produce the final article."
    ),
    sub_agents=[researcher, writer],
)
The orchestrator generates text that invokes sub-agents by name, passing the task as a string. ADK routes the call to the named sub-agent and injects the result back into the orchestrator's context.
for local development; swap to a persistent backend (Cloud Spanner, Firestore) for production.
. The runner appends every turn to the session automatically, so the next call continues the conversation.
Callbacks intercept the agent lifecycle at specific points. Return
to pass through, or return a replacement value to override.
Deploying to Vertex AI Agent Engine
ADK integrates with Vertex AI Agent Engine for managed scaling, logging, and monitoring.

# After developing locally, deploy to Vertex AI Agent Engine

from vertexai.preview import reasoning_engines

# Wrap the ADK app in an AdkApp
from google.adk.agents.adk_app import AdkApp

adk_app = AdkApp(agent=orchestrator, enable_tracing=True)

# Deploy
remote_app = reasoning_engines.ReasoningEngine.create(
    adk_app,
    requirements=["google-adk", "google-cloud-aiplatform"],
    display_name="My ADK Orchestrator",
    project="my-gcp-project",
    location="us-central1",
)

# Invoke the deployed agent
response = remote_app.query(
    user_id="user1",
    message="Research and write a short article about quantum computing.",
)
print(response)
Agent Engine provisions a managed runtime; you pay per request. For cost-sensitive workloads, profile token usage locally with
The runner emits typed events during each turn. Understanding them lets you display partial output, handle tool results, or trace execution.
